explanation:
The requirement states that when the expiratory pause timeout ('max exp pause') is over, the cycle should immediately proceed to inspiration. This implies that at the moment when the expiratory pause ends, the system should enter the inspiratory phase without delay. The atomic propositions used are 'expiratoryPause' (indicates Expiratory Pause) and 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' (indicates start of inspiratory phase). The property thus needs to ensure that whenever 'expiratoryPause' transitions from true to false (i.e., it is over), 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' holds in the very next step. Template 20 captures this pattern: '((G (((! bool_exp2) & (X bool_exp2)) -> (X bool_exp3))) & (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3))', where 'bool_exp2' is 'expiratoryPause' (the condition being watched for a falling edge) and 'bool_exp3' is 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' (the required action that must occur immediately after).

This template guarantees that any time 'expiratoryPause' ends, 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' is triggered at the next step, matching the immediate transition required in the natural language specification.

The requirement states that when the expiratory pause timeout ("max exp pause") is over, the ventilatory cycle should immediately move to inspiration. In the atomic propositions, 'expiratoryPause' indicates the active expiratory pause state, and 'inspiratoryPhase' indicates the system is in inspiration. The phrase "when ... is over, ... shall proceed immediately" matches the pattern where upon the transition (falling edge) from expiratoryPause being true to false, inspiratoryPhase should become true in the next step. This is best captured by the template that enforces: whenever expiratoryPause ceases to be true (i.e., falling edge), in the next time step, inspiratoryPhase must be true. Hence, template 60 is selected.

The requirement states that when the expiratory pause timeout (max exp pause) is over, the system must immediately proceed to inspiration. From the propositions, 'expiratoryPause' indicates the expiratory pause state, and 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' signals the start of the inspiratory phase. The common CTL/LTL style to capture 'when A ends, do B' is to say: whenever 'not expiratoryPause' (i.e., we are NOT in expiratory pause), then the inspiratory phase start must happen immediately. Thus, we use template 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp2 is '!expiratoryPause' and bool_exp3 is 'inspiratoryPhaseStart'. This ensures that on any transition where we exit expiratory pause (i.e., 'expiratoryPause' is now false), the very next state must see 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' true. This captures the immediate transition on 'expiratoryPause' ending.

The property captures that whenever the expiratory pause is deasserted/ends (i.e., exp pause timeout is over), then on the next transition (immediately), inspiration phase must start (the cycle proceeds to inspiration). This is modeled by detecting when 'expiratoryPause' becomes false (it was true, then false at the next step), and requiring that 'inspiratoryPhase' follows immediately. This uses a template for 'when a condition becomes false, another should become true the next instant'.

The requirement states that once the maximum expiratory pause timeout is over, the ventilator must immediately proceed to the inspiration phase. The atomic proposition 'expiratoryPause' indicates that the expiratory pause is active, and 'inspiratoryPhase' indicates that the inspiration phase is active. To capture this timing relationship, we encode that whenever the expiratory pause ceases to be true (i.e., it ends), the next state (immediately after) must be the inspiration phase. The appropriate template for this is template 60, which ensures that upon a rising edge (transition from false to true) for expiratoryPause, the required state transition occurs in the next step. In this case, we track the end of expiratoryPause (falling edge), so we reverse the logic: When 'expiratoryPause' goes from true to false, 'inspiratoryPhase' must be true in the next step.

The specification requires that immediately after the expiratory pause ends, the cycle must proceed to inspiration right away. In the atomic propositions, 'expiratoryPause' indicates the pause is ongoing, and 'inspiratoryPhase' indicates the start of the inspiration phase. The most direct way to enforce this is to state: whenever 'expiratoryPause' becomes false (i.e., is not true at time t), then 'inspiratoryPhase' must be true in the next time step (at time t+1). In LTL, this can be written using the template (G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))), where bool_exp2 is (!expiratoryPause) (i.e., the pause is not active), and bool_exp3 is inspiratoryPhase (i.e., inspiration starts). This means: globally, whenever the system is not in expiratory pause, in the next step, inspiratoryPhase holds. This adequately captures the desired synchronous transition from the end of expiratory pause to inspiration as specified.

The property describes that whenever the expiratory pause ends (i.e., when 'expiratoryPause' becomes false in the following state), the inspiration phase must immediately begin (i.e., 'inspiratoryPhase' is true in the next state). This matches the requirement that once the expiratory pause timeout is over, the cycle should proceed without delay to inspiration.

The natural language requirement states that after the expiratory pause timeout is over (max exp pause), the ventilator must proceed immediately to inspiration. From the atomic propositions, 'expiratoryPause' indicates an expiratory pause is active, and 'inspiratoryPhase' indicates the inspiratory phase is active. This means that after expiratoryPause ends (i.e., it is false at this instant), the next state must be inspiratoryPhase (i.e., inspiration starts immediately). This behavior is best captured by the LTL pattern: Globally, whenever 'expiratoryPause' changes from true to false (i.e., (!expiratoryPause) & X(expiratoryPause)), in the next step 'inspiratoryPhase' must be true. This matches template 20.

The property formalizes the requirement that whenever the system exits the expiratory pause (i.e., 'expiratoryPause' is true and becomes false in the next state), the system must proceed immediately to the inspiration phase (i.e., 'inspiratoryPhase' is true in the next state). This is done by stating: whenever 'expiratoryPause' is true and in the next state it is not ('!expiratoryPause'), then in the next state ('X'), 'inspiratoryPhase' must hold. This precisely captures the requirement for immediate transition from expiratory pause to inspiration.

The requirement is: when the expiratory pause (max exp pause) ends, the cycle shall proceed immediately to inspiration. In terms of atomic propositions, 'expiratoryPause' is true during the pause, and turns false when the pause ends. 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' marks the start of inspiration. The most direct translation is to say that any time 'expiratoryPause' becomes false (i.e., '(!expiratoryPause)'), then 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' must occur immediately (as the next event). The most appropriate template is template 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)).

Here, bool_exp2 = (!expiratoryPause) (expiratoryPause is false), and bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPhaseStart (i.e., the transition to inspiration). This expresses that whenever the pause has ended at any state, then at the next step inspiration must start immediately.
